page: Microsoft Azure M416s_9_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 416 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 208 physical cores of Intel Xeon 8280L processors operating at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 8568.0 GB, providing approximately 20.6 GB of RAM per core, and includes 4194 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core performance is highly robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is also exceptional, reaching 20894.32 GB/sec for cached reads and 746.92 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

Given its massive memory footprint and high multi-threaded throughput, this server is highly efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy database operations, and static web serving, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis SET operations at 353.37 million operations per second and web throughput of 110.97 GB/sec. While prompt processing for large LLM models is strong, text generation speeds are poor due to the lack of hardware acceleration. The qualitative cost profile reflects a premium, high-density resource allocation designed for enterprise-grade workloads that require massive RAM consolidation rather than high single-threaded clock speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure M416s_9_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the MSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by Intel Xeon 8280L processors, providing 416 dedicated vCPUs across 208 physical cores at 2.7 GHz. The server features a massive 8568.0 GB of RAM and 4194 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability, but lacks dedicated GPUs. While single-core benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks perform poorly, the instance achieves top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 20894.32 GB/sec for cached reads. It is highly optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis operations, and static web serving, though it is less suited for tasks requiring high single-threaded clock speeds or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M416s_9_v2 is an MSv2 family memory-optimized server featuring 416 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8568 GB of RAM, and 4194 GB of local storage, designed for massive database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M416s_9_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 416 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.5 TB of RAM, and 4.1 TB of local storage.
tagline: A massive memory-optimized instance featuring 416 vCPUs and 8.5 TB of RAM for enterprise database and in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 416 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8568 GB of RAM.
4194 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth.
Excellent throughput for Redis and database operations.
Weak single-core performance and slow LLM text generation.
